prefer-numpy-array.description: PY001: Prefer NumPy Array over standard lists for numerical data.

string-concatenation-in-loops.description: PY002: Use join() instead of + for strings in loops.

excessive-global-variables.description: PY003: Avoid using too many global variables.

use-zip-for-multiple-lists.description: PY004: Use zip() to iterate over multiple iterables.

use-explicit-typing.description: PY005: Use Python type hints for better readability.

iterate-with-dict-items.description: PY007: Use dict.items() to get both keys and values.

avoid-mutable-default-args.description: PY010: Do not use [] or {} as default function arguments.

always-return-consistent-type.description: PY011: Ensure functions don't return mixed types (e.g., None AND Int).

prefer-namedtuple-dataclass.description: PY012: Use dataclasses for structured data.

avoid-is-for-value-comparison.description: PY013: Use == instead of 'is' for value equality.

use-set-for-membership-tests.description: PY014: Sets are O(1) for 'in' checks, lists are O(n).

use-any-all.description: PY017: Use any() and all() for cleaner boolean logic.

avoid-uncontrolled-memory-growth.description: PY018: Watch out for leaking memory in long-running processes.

avoid-nested-loops.description: PY019: Highly nested loops kill performance.

prefer-generators-over-temporary-lists.description: PY020: Use (x for x in y) instead of [x for x in y] for large data.

avoid-rebuilding-lists-with-plus.description: PY021: Use .append() or .extend() instead of list + list.

prefer-for-over-manual-while.description: PY022: Use 'for' loops for standard iteration.

use-with-to-open-files.description: PY024: Use Context Managers to ensure files close.

use-isinstance-for-type-checking.description: PY025: Use isinstance(x, int) instead of type(x) == int.

use-dict-comprehensions.description: PY026: {k: v for ...} is cleaner than dict() constructor.

use-unpacking-for-multiple-assignments.description: PY027: Use a, b = b, a for swapping or multi-assignment.

avoid-type-in-variable-names.description: PY028: Don't name a variable 'user_list'; use 'users'.

order-except-clauses-correctly.description: PY029: Catch specific exceptions before generic ones.

reduce-python-object-cost.description: PY030: Use __slots__ to save memory on objects.

prefer-eafp-over-lbyl.description: PY031: Easier to Ask Forgiveness than Permission (try/except).

prefer-list-comprehensions-over-map-filt.description: PY032: [x for x in y] is more 'Pythonic' than map().

prefer-set-for-membership-checks.description: PY033: Similar to PY014; prefer set for fast lookup.

favor-memory-locality.description: PY034: Structure data for better cache performance.
